title: Universe As Verb
definition: The philosophical perspective that reality is not composed of static objects but a dynamic, interconnected process.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Imagine the universe as a story unfolding, rather than a collection of fixed objects. Everything changes and evolves over time, just like a narrative.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The Universe-as-Verb perspective proposes that reality is best understood as an ever-changing, interconnected process, not as a collection of static objects or entities.
difficulty_levels.expert: The Universe-as-Verb perspective posits that the universe is better described as a verb rather than a noun, dissolving the illusion of a separate observer and framing all phenomena as active expressions of a unified field.

extra_fields.insight: By viewing the universe as a verb, we can reconcile the rigor of physics with the wonder of metaphysics, understanding that everything is part of an interconnected process of existence.
